description_en: A tangible cultural property classified as a building, a former bridge. It is municipally designated by Fukushima City.

name_en: "Revolving Sutra Repository" of Kannonji Temple in Kamitorido
description_en: A tangible cultural property classified as a building, a revolving sutra repository (rinzō). It is municipally designated by Fukushima City.

name_en: Shrine Buildings of Iizaka Hachiman Shrine (Main Hall, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall)
description_en: A tangible cultural property classified as a building, comprising the main hall, worship hall, and offering hall of Iizaka Hachiman Shrine. It is municipally designated by Fukushima City.

name_romaji: Moto Kyakujiken Kyū Higashimune oyobi Kyū Kitamune
name_en: Former East and North Wings of the Former Kyakujiken
description_en: A tangible cultural property classified as a building, comprising the former east and north wings of the Kyakujiken. It is municipally designated by Fukushima City.

name_romaji: Kyū Nihon Denki Keiki Kenteijo Fukushima Shikenjo Shaoku
name_en: Former Office Building of the Fukushima Testing Station of the Japan Electric Meters Inspection Corporation
description_en: A tangible cultural property classified as a building, a former office building of the Fukushima testing station. It is municipally designated by Fukushima City.
A tangible cultural property classified as a building, a former dwelling of the Baba family. It is municipally designated by Fukushima City.

name_romaji: Ōkuraji Kannondō Okunoin (Tsuketari) Moto・Dōin-nai Suetsuke no Shumidan, Munafuda
name_en: Inner Sanctuary of the Kannon Hall of Ōkuraji Temple, with Former Altar Platform and Ridge Tablet
description_en: A tangible cultural property classified as a building, the inner sanctuary of the Kannon hall together with the former altar platform and ridge tablet. It is municipally designated by Fukushima City.

name_romaji: Kyū Horikiri-ke Komegura (Tsūshō Jikkengura) (Tsuketari) Munafuda, Hyakushōdame
name_en: Former Horikiri Family Rice Storehouse (Commonly the Ten-Bay Storehouse), with Ridge Tablet and Peasants' Waiting Room
description_en: A tangible cultural property classified as a building, the former rice storehouse of the Horikiri family with a ridge tablet and a peasants' waiting room. It is municipally designated by Fukushima City.

name_en: Wooden Replica of the Ishimoda Memorial Stone Stupa
description_en: A tangible cultural property classified as a historical material, a wooden replica of the Ishimoda memorial stone stupa. It is municipally designated by Fukushima City.

name_romaji: Itakura Jinja Shozō Shiryō narabini Jūmotsu 102-ten
name_en: 102 Materials and Treasures Held by Itakura Shrine
description_en: A tangible cultural property classified as a historical material, a set of 102 materials and treasures held by Itakura Shrine. It is municipally designated by Fukushima City.

name_en: Hanging Scrolls Brushed by Ryūzenshi (2 Scrolls)

description_en: A cultural property in the 'other' category, a set of 36 scrolls depicting the Thirty-Six Immortal Poets, formerly designated by the old Iino Town. It is municipally designated by Fukushima City.

description_en: An intangible folk cultural property, a kenzensai food-offering festival. It is municipally designated by Fukushima City.

description_en: An intangible folk cultural property, the traditional technique of making Tsuchiyu kokeshi dolls. It is municipally designated by Fukushima City.
